_meta.note: AUTHORITATIVE content source. All four complete decks use the closed vocabularies below. Numeric values may be tuned by the Phase 1 simulation; effect types require a versioned rules change.
_meta.disruptionTiming: A disruption is drawn at year-end Y5, revealed publicly for year N+1 (Admin L3 sees year N+2), and its effects attach to the full following academic year (all 5 rounds plus that year's year-end sequence) unless the effect says otherwise.

effectTypeVocabulary.poolMultiplier: multiplies the annual applicant pool for the year
effectTypeVocabulary.upkeepMultiplier: multiplies all upkeep, applied after cost disease, before Admin L4 discount
effectTypeVocabulary.yieldMultiplier: multiplies all recruiting yields (admissions, campaign, program), applied after floors, capped at 1.0
effectTypeVocabulary.campaignSpendCap: overrides every player's campaign spend cap to min(levelCap, value)
effectTypeVocabulary.reputationPullExponentDelta: +1 raises every reputation pull multiplier (rep/50) to the next power; programs with their own exponent override (Business) are unaffected
effectTypeVocabulary.athleticsPayoutMultiplier: multiplies Great season gains and/or Losing season losses (money and reputation; bonus conversions unchanged)
effectTypeVocabulary.strainFirstOffensePenalty: one-time reputation penalty the first round a player is over Academics capacity this year, in addition to normal strain
effectTypeVocabulary.raceReward: one-time reward to the FIRST player meeting the condition this year; simultaneous qualification broken by priority token proximity at the moment of qualification
effectTypeVocabulary.programRider: conditional modifier applying only to players holding the tagged program
effectTypeVocabulary.programOpenCostMultiplier: multiplies the open cost of a tagged program for the year
effectTypeVocabulary.poachModifier: multiplies poach cost and/or poach fraction for the year
effectTypeVocabulary.donationMultiplier: multiplies year-end alumni donation income (base + Engineering bonus; not Public Affairs grants)

playerCardRules.targeting: Every Fortune/Crisis card declares target: either a fixed department or 'random'. Decks are shuffled and drawn from the top without selection RNG. The engine ALWAYS consumes exactly one target RNG draw, discarding it for fixed-target cards.
playerCardRules.scaling: Effects marked scalable multiply by the fortune formula (targetLevel+1)/3 or crisis formula (6−targetLevel)/5. Non-scalable effects apply as written. Retention effects remain subject to the 0.92 cap after scaling.

playerCardEffectVocabulary.bonusConversionsThisRound: students added immediately during Chance, outside pool scaling, before strain and elimination; scalable
playerCardEffectVocabulary.bonusConversionsNextRound: same, queued for next round, scalable
playerCardEffectVocabulary.retentionDeltaThisYear: adds to this year's retention rate, scalable, 0.92 cap applies
playerCardEffectVocabulary.campaignYieldFloorBonusNext: raises the yield floor of the player's next campaign, not scalable
playerCardEffectVocabulary.campaignYieldLockNext: next campaign yield fixed at value, not scalable
playerCardEffectVocabulary.campaignPullMultiplierNext: next campaign pull multiplied, not scalable
playerCardEffectVocabulary.campaignBlockedNextRound: player may not campaign next round (crisis), not scalable
playerCardEffectVocabulary.upkeepRefundFraction: refund fraction of this round's paid upkeep, not scalable
playerCardEffectVocabulary.temporaryCapacityThisYear: Academics capacity bonus for this year only, scalable
playerCardEffectVocabulary.donationMultiplierThisYearEnd: multiplies this year-end's alumni donations, not scalable
playerCardEffectVocabulary.nextCrisisSeverityReduction: player's next crisis resolves at reduced severity (min 1), not scalable
playerCardEffectVocabulary.extraActionsNextRound: additional allocation actions next round, not scalable
playerCardEffectVocabulary.treasuryRevealedRounds: exact treasury shown publicly for N rounds (crisis), not scalable
playerCardEffectVocabulary.recruitingPenaltyNextRound: multiplies NEXT round's total conversions (crisis; crises resolve after recruiting, so same-round penalties are impossible — see resolution-order.md Step 6 vs Step 8), not scalable
playerCardEffectVocabulary.programRider: bonus applying only if the named program is held, never scaled

annualDisruptions.name: The Cliff Steepens
annualDisruptions.flavor: A demographer publishes a chart. The chart goes down. Everyone shares the chart. The chart does not care.
annualDisruptions.prepHint: Retention is recruiting you already paid for. Student Affairs and Education hold the line; Nursing doesn't even read the chart.

annualDisruptions.name: State Budget 'Realignment'
annualDisruptions.flavor: The governor assures higher education that it remains a top priority, alongside the eleven other top priorities announced this morning.
annualDisruptions.prepHint: Bank cash, trim bloat before it starts, or let Admin L4's consultants earn their keep.

annualDisruptions.name: Rankings Methodology Overhaul
annualDisruptions.flavor: The magazine has changed its formula. It will not say how. It will say that your number went down.
annualDisruptions.prepHint: Reputation now compounds: (rep/50) becomes (rep/50)². Above 50 you're amplified; below 50 you're invisible. Pump reputation the year before — or hide behind Nursing, which never reads the rankings.

annualDisruptions.name: Financial Aid Portal 'Modernization'
annualDisruptions.flavor: The new form is simpler. It is also unavailable. Families are advised to try again during a full moon.
annualDisruptions.prepHint: Every yield takes a 20% haircut and big Marketing pushes are pointless. A stable Admissions floor is the whole game this year.

annualDisruptions.name: Conference Realignment
annualDisruptions.flavor: Your century-old rivalry is dissolved effective immediately. Your new rival is nine hundred miles away and has a television deal.
annualDisruptions.prepHint: Athletics stakes double in both directions. Go big before the season, or divest and watch the carnage from the quad.

annualDisruptions.name: Accreditation Review Year
annualDisruptions.flavor: The site visit team has arrived. They would like to see documentation. They would like to see all of the documentation.
annualDisruptions.prepHint: Being over Academics capacity even once this year costs 10 Reputation on the spot. Fix your ratios in advance — the reviewers do not accept vibes.

annualDisruptions.name: The Viral Moment
annualDisruptions.flavor: A student's campus tour video has 40 million views. Nobody knows why. Every marketing office in the region is trying to know why.
annualDisruptions.prepHint: First university to hit 65 Reputation this year catches the wave: +600 students. A race rewards those who started running last year.

annualDisruptions.name: National Nursing Shortage
annualDisruptions.flavor: The hospital system three exits down will hire literally anyone you graduate. They have asked us to italicize 'literally.'
annualDisruptions.prepHint: Nursing holders feast (+100 pull/round). Everyone else can open Nursing at half price this year — a one-year door into the cliff-proof business.

annualDisruptions.name: Tech Sector Boom
annualDisruptions.flavor: A startup founded in your rival's dorm just sold for two billion dollars. Their commencement speaker is booked through 2031.
annualDisruptions.prepHint: Business pull +25%, Engineering's alumni bonus doubles. If you hold neither, this is the year your rivals' brochures write themselves.

annualDisruptions.name: The Universal Promise Scholarship
annualDisruptions.flavor: The legislature has made college more affordable by making it less fundable. Applications are up. Revenue is not.
annualDisruptions.prepHint: More students, each worth less. A volume year: capacity and retention builds cash in; boutique high-cost builds tighten their belts.

annualDisruptions.name: Merger Mania
annualDisruptions.flavor: The consulting firm's report recommends 'strategic consolidation.' The consulting firm bills by the acquisition.
annualDisruptions.prepHint: Poaching costs half and takes double. A knives-out year: don't be the wounded one, and if a rival is bleeding — well. The report did recommend it.

annualDisruptions.name: The Charitable Deduction Window
annualDisruptions.flavor: A quirk in the new tax code makes this the best year in a generation to give. Development officers describe the mood as 'feral.'
annualDisruptions.prepHint: Alumni donations double at year-end. Every graduate you produced before now pays twice — the Prestige build's harvest festival.

fortuneCards.name: The Counselor Whisperer
fortuneCards.flavor: A high school counselor two counties over has decided you are 'the good one.' She has opinions and a mailing list.

fortuneCards.name: The Yield Model Actually Works
fortuneCards.flavor: The predictive model was right. Institutional Research has printed the email and framed it.

fortuneCards.name: Legacy Family Reunion
fortuneCards.flavor: The Hendersons have sent a fourth generation, a fifth generation, and a check.

fortuneCards.name: Common App Glitch (In Your Favor)
fortuneCards.flavor: A dropdown menu error routed three districts' worth of applications to you. Legal says keep them.

fortuneCards.name: 60 Minutes Profiles Your Access Mission
fortuneCards.flavor: Twelve minutes of prime time about first-generation students. The anchor cried. The phones have not stopped.

fortuneCards.name: Billboard Placement Luck
fortuneCards.flavor: Your billboard is now directly across from your rival's billboard, which is advertising a parking amnesty.

fortuneCards.name: Campaign Goes Viral
fortuneCards.flavor: The intern's 'unhinged' idea worked. The intern would like to discuss compensation.

fortuneCards.name: Agency Comps the Retainer
fortuneCards.flavor: The agency made an 'attribution error' in your favor and would prefer you not ask follow-up questions.

fortuneCards.name: Mascot Meme Season
fortuneCards.flavor: The mascot fell down the library steps with tremendous dignity. The internet has adopted him.

fortuneCards.name: Super Bowl Slot at Local Rates
fortuneCards.flavor: A regional ad buy error means your thirty seconds airs nationally. The agency is 'looking into it' from a beach.

fortuneCards.name: Star Faculty Hire
fortuneCards.flavor: She chose you over three flagships because of 'the vibe.' Her grant portfolio arrives Tuesday.

fortuneCards.name: Nobel Shortlist Rumor
fortuneCards.flavor: Nobody can confirm it. Nobody is trying very hard to deny it. The press release is 'ready either way.'

fortuneCards.name: Federal Grant Renewal
fortuneCards.flavor: Five more years of funding, awarded specifically for the section of the proposal nobody proofread.

fortuneCards.name: Curriculum Featured in the Times
fortuneCards.flavor: A columnist calls your core curriculum 'what college is supposed to be.' Deans nationwide are 'circulating it.'

fortuneCards.name: Visiting Scholar Draws Crowds
fortuneCards.flavor: Her seminar has a waitlist, a livestream, and a bouncer.

fortuneCards.name: Dining Hall Wins an Award
fortuneCards.flavor: 'Best Campus Dining, Regional' — a category that exists and now belongs to you.

fortuneCards.name: Retention Program Featured Nationally
fortuneCards.flavor: Your early-alert system is the subject of a webinar attended by every university that will now copy it badly.

fortuneCards.name: Therapy Dogs, But More
fortuneCards.flavor: The finals-week puppy room now has a llama. Attendance metrics are 'off the charts and also adorable.'

fortuneCards.name: Housing Bond Refinanced
fortuneCards.flavor: The CFO caught a rate window and has been insufferable about it for three weeks.

fortuneCards.name: The RA Who Saved Homecoming
fortuneCards.flavor: Power outage, rainstorm, one resident assistant with a bullhorn and a plan. There is already a mural.

fortuneCards.name: Booster Whale
fortuneCards.flavor: He made his fortune in regional waste management and he loves one (1) volleyball team.

fortuneCards.name: Walk-On Cinderella Story
fortuneCards.flavor: She tried out on a dare. She is now the conference player of the week and your best recruiter.

fortuneCards.name: Merch Licensing Windfall
fortuneCards.flavor: The retro logo is back and inexplicably beloved by people who have never attended a game.

fortuneCards.name: ESPN Feature Segment
fortuneCards.flavor: Four minutes of slow-motion footage and a voiceover about 'heart.' Admissions traffic spikes immediately.

fortuneCards.name: Season Ticket Miracle
fortuneCards.flavor: The waitlist has a waitlist. The waitlist's waitlist has a newsletter.

fortuneCards.name: Clean Audit
fortuneCards.flavor: Zero findings. The auditors seem almost disappointed. One of them lingers, hoping.

fortuneCards.name: Insurance Settlement Arrives
fortuneCards.flavor: The claim from three years ago has settled. Nobody remembers filing it. The check clears anyway.

fortuneCards.name: Grant Writer on a Heater
fortuneCards.flavor: Four submissions, four awards. She has asked that no one speak to her, look at her, or change anything about her desk.

fortuneCards.name: Emergency Drill Goes Perfectly
fortuneCards.flavor: The tabletop exercise revealed no gaps, which the consultants note has 'never happened before, anywhere.'

fortuneCards.name: The Provost's Masterstroke
fortuneCards.flavor: Nobody knows exactly what she did at that conference. Three initiatives unblocked themselves by Friday.

fortuneCards.name: Unrestricted Bequest
fortuneCards.flavor: The will says the money goes to 'whatever they're doing best over there.' The lawyers have interpreted generously.

fortuneCards.name: Puff Piece
fortuneCards.flavor: The local paper needed a feel-good story and you needed the local paper. Everyone wins, journalism included.

fortuneCards.name: The Grateful Graduate
fortuneCards.flavor: He credits one specific office with 'turning his life around' and has attached a number to that feeling.

fortuneCards.name: Departmental Newsletter Goes Unreasonably Hard
fortuneCards.flavor: Whoever writes it has found their voice, and their voice is 'beloved regional institution.'

fortuneCards.name: The Forgotten Fund
fortuneCards.flavor: An endowment from 1961, restricted to a purpose that — upon review — is exactly what this department does.

fortuneCards.name: The Photogenic Quad
fortuneCards.flavor: The light hit the bell tower. A tour group gasped. Seventeen deposits followed within the hour.

crisisCards.name: The Side Door
crisisCards.flavor: A celebrity's child was admitted as a coxswain. The university does not have a rowing team. It has never had a rowing team.

crisisCards.name: The Wrong Envelope
crisisCards.flavor: Five hundred acceptance letters went out in error. The rescission emails went out on a Friday. The screenshots went out immediately.

crisisCards.name: Tent City
crisisCards.flavor: Move-in day exceeded projections. The quad now features 'temporary residential structures' and a photographer from the AP.

crisisCards.name: The FERPA Fumble
crisisCards.flavor: The mail merge attached the spreadsheet. The spreadsheet was the whole spreadsheet.

crisisCards.name: Summer Melt
crisisCards.flavor: The deposited class has been quietly re-evaluating its options, its finances, and your parking situation.

crisisCards.name: Ad Buy Backfires
crisisCards.flavor: Your pre-roll ad ran, exclusively and repeatedly, before a documentary about student debt.

crisisCards.name: The Stock Photo Incident
crisisCards.flavor: The new viewbook's joyful students have been identified. They attend your rival. They are wearing your rival's hoodie.

crisisCards.name: The Hashtag Gets Hijacked
crisisCards.flavor: #WhyIChose[U] was meant to inspire. It has instead become a genre.

crisisCards.name: Typo on the Water Tower
crisisCards.flavor: It's been there for six weeks. An alumnus noticed from an airplane.

crisisCards.name: Influencer Contract Meltdown
crisisCards.flavor: The campus ambassador failed to disclose the sponsorship, then disclosed several other things instead.

crisisCards.name: The Tenured Tirade
crisisCards.flavor: The lecture was recorded. The recording was posted. The phrase 'academic freedom' is doing more work than it was built for.

crisisCards.name: Conduct Unbecoming
crisisCards.flavor: The investigation substantiated the complaints. The settlement is confidential. The confidentiality is not going well.

crisisCards.name: The Grade Inflation Exposé
crisisCards.flavor: An analysis reveals the median GPA is 3.94. The registrar describes this as 'a strong cohort, nineteen years running.'

crisisCards.name: Accreditation Paperwork Warning
crisisCards.flavor: A formal notice regarding the timely submission of the form confirming the submission of the forms.

crisisCards.name: Adjunct Exodus
crisisCards.flavor: Four adjuncts have accepted positions at a warehouse offering, in their words, 'benefits.'

crisisCards.name: Housing Mold Outbreak
crisisCards.flavor: Residents of the third floor describe the ceiling as 'textured' and 'expanding.'

crisisCards.name: The Parking Uprising (Peaceful)
crisisCards.flavor: Students have organized. Their demands are reasonable, their signage is excellent, and their grievance is eternal.

crisisCards.name: Homecoming Weekend Incident
crisisCards.flavor: The official report uses the word 'regrettable' eleven times and the word 'bonfire' zero times, which is itself notable.

crisisCards.name: Dining Contract Dispute
crisisCards.flavor: Negotiations have stalled. The menu has not changed in forty days. The students are keeping count publicly.

crisisCards.name: Residence Hall Condemned
crisisCards.flavor: The engineering assessment contains the phrase 'load-bearing in spirit.'

crisisCards.name: Hazing Scandal
crisisCards.flavor: The team-building exercise was neither team-building nor, per counsel, an exercise.

crisisCards.name: Transfer Portal Raid
crisisCards.flavor: Your starting lineup has entered the portal. The portal, as ever, only goes one direction.

crisisCards.name: The Coach Buyout
crisisCards.flavor: He is being paid $3 million to not coach, which observers note is consistent with his prior work.

crisisCards.name: Equipment Recall
crisisCards.flavor: The helmets have been recalled. The invoice for the replacement helmets has not been recalled.

crisisCards.name: NCAA Investigation
crisisCards.flavor: The notice of allegations is 84 pages. The boosters have gone quiet, which the boosters have never once done.

crisisCards.name: Data Breach
crisisCards.flavor: The attackers gained access through an invoice titled 'INVOICE (REAL).' The forensics report is unsparing.

crisisCards.name: The Consultants Return
crisisCards.flavor: Their final report recommends a follow-up engagement to assess the implementation of the previous report.

crisisCards.name: Compliance Audit Findings
crisisCards.flavor: Seventeen findings, twelve repeat findings, and one finding about the process for tracking findings.

crisisCards.name: ERP Migration 'Go-Live'
crisisCards.flavor: The new system is live. Payroll is 'substantially live.' The old system has been decommissioned out of an abundance of optimism.

crisisCards.name: The Embezzlement
crisisCards.flavor: Fourteen years. Small amounts. A beloved figure. A boat named 'Fiscal Year.'

crisisCards.name: The Burst Pipe
crisisCards.flavor: Facilities describes the building's plumbing as 'original, in every sense.'

crisisCards.name: Poached by a Flagship
crisisCards.flavor: The person who actually ran that office has taken a job with a title, a raise, and — witnesses report — free parking.

crisisCards.name: The Settlement
crisisCards.flavor: Counsel advises that this outcome, while painful, is 'better than discovery.' Counsel declines to elaborate.

crisisCards.name: The Anonymous Op-Ed
crisisCards.flavor: 'I Work There, and I Have Concerns' — published, shared, and printed out by at least one trustee.

crisisCards.name: The Whistleblower
crisisCards.flavor: The documents are real. The binder is comprehensive. The tabs are color-coded, which somehow makes it worse.

crisisCards.name: The Phishing Test
crisisCards.flavor: IT ran a simulated phishing campaign. The click-through rate has been described as 'enthusiastic.'

headlineRules.scope: Every effect applies to ALL surviving players identically, this round only. No headline may exceed half the impact of a severity-1 card. No-op fraction ≈ 20%.

effectVocabulary.departmentUpkeepMultiplier: multiplies one named department's upkeep this round, for everyone
effectVocabulary.allPullMultiplier: multiplies every pull class this round (Nursing included — weather affects everyone)
effectVocabulary.programPullMultiplier: multiplies one named program's pull this round, for all holders
effectVocabulary.yieldMultiplier: multiplies all yields this round, capped at 1.0
effectVocabulary.reputationDeltaAll: reputation delta for every player (±1 max)
effectVocabulary.moneyDeltaAll: flat treasury delta for every player (±0.5 max)
effectVocabulary.programMoneyDelta: flat treasury delta for holders of the named program

headlines.name: Tuition Discount War Escalates
headlines.flavor: A rival announces a 'Presidential Opportunity Scholarship.' Everyone announces a 'Presidential Opportunity Scholarship.'

headlines.name: Gen Z Suddenly Loves College Again
headlines.flavor: A survey finds young people value higher education. The survey is laminated and distributed at three separate cabinet meetings.

headlines.name: Statewide Ransomware Wave
headlines.flavor: Every IT office in the region is 'operating in a heightened posture,' which is expensive posture.

headlines.name: 'Day in My Life' Trend Hits Campus Content
headlines.flavor: The algorithm has decided dining halls are cinema. Marketing offices scramble to look effortless.

headlines.name: National Coaching Carousel
headlines.flavor: Every coach is rumored for every job. The agents bill hourly for the rumors.

headlines.name: Slow News Week
headlines.flavor: Nothing happened. Everyone exhales.

headlines.name: Utility Rates Spike
headlines.flavor: The grid operator cites 'seasonal factors,' as it does in all four seasons.

headlines.name: Mild Winter
headlines.flavor: Facilities reports the heating budget is 'weirdly fine.' No one trusts it.

headlines.name: Rankings Season Anxiety
headlines.flavor: The lists drop next month. Families nationwide have decided to wait and see, together, all at once.

headlines.name: College Fair Circuit Weekend
headlines.flavor: Four hundred folding tables. Nine thousand tote bags. One shared destiny.

headlines.name: Recession Fears (Again)
headlines.flavor: Economists are divided. Families are not.

headlines.name: Nurses Featured on Every Morning Show
headlines.flavor: A week of segments about 'the heroes we need.' The heroes need clinical placements.

headlines.name: Crypto Commencement Speaker Backlash
headlines.flavor: He spoke about disruption for forty minutes. He has since been disrupted.

headlines.name: STEM Hiring Headlines
headlines.flavor: A chip plant broke ground two hours away and every guidance counselor felt it.

headlines.name: Teacher Pay Coverage Week
headlines.flavor: A viral pay-stub screenshot does what forty years of white papers could not, which is also depress everyone.

headlines.name: The Humanities, Defended Beautifully
headlines.flavor: The essay is magnificent. It is shared by everyone, including several people who have read it.

headlines.name: State Capitol Photo Op Day
headlines.flavor: Legislators pose with oversized checks. The checks, unusually, are real, if not oversized.

headlines.name: Enrollment Cliff Think Piece #4,081
headlines.flavor: It says what the other 4,080 said, but with a new chart color.

headlines.name: 'Campus Is Back' Local News Segment
headlines.flavor: B-roll of frisbee. B-roll of lecture. B-roll of a squirrel the anchor calls 'the real dean.'

headlines.name: Gas Prices
headlines.flavor: Somehow, this affects everything, including several things it should not.

headlines.name: A Senator Discovers Deans
headlines.flavor: A thread about administrative bloat, posted from a phone held by a staff of fourteen.

headlines.name: Graduation Story Goes National
headlines.flavor: A 74-year-old grandmother finishes her degree and the entire country is briefly nice about higher education.

headlines.name: FAFSA Works Correctly All Week
headlines.flavor: Financial aid offices describe the mood as 'suspicious.'

headlines.name: Test-Optional Discourse Reignites
headlines.flavor: Positions are restated. Nothing changes. A panel is convened to discuss the panel.

headlines.name: Bookstore Buyback Season
headlines.flavor: The margins on branded fleece remain the healthiest thing on campus.

headlines.name: Surprise Facilities Assessments
headlines.flavor: A regional inspection sweep finds that every campus has 'deferred maintenance,' a phrase meaning 'maintenance.'

headlines.name: 'Is College Worth It?' Podcast Episode
headlines.flavor: It has 400 listens. Four of them are your board.

headlines.name: Merger Rumors Swirl
headlines.flavor: The consulting firms are circling. Due diligence has never been this affordable.

headlines.name: Accreditors Announce New Framework
headlines.flavor: The comment period is open. The comment period will be extended. The comment period outlives us all.

headlines.name: Alumni Magazine Actually Read This Month
headlines.flavor: The class notes section mentioned a boat. Envelopes followed.

headlines.name: Rain Ruins Every Campus Tour
headlines.flavor: The tour guides walk backward through the puddles with unbroken smiles. It is not enough.

headlines.name: Perfect Foliage Weekend
headlines.flavor: The campus photographs itself. Deposits arrive attached to leaf emojis.

headlines.name: National Championship Ad Rates
headlines.flavor: The big game has priced every regional advertiser out of the market, including you, especially you.

headlines.name: Dorm Tour Videos Trend
headlines.flavor: Prospective students now evaluate institutions primarily by natural light and outlet count.

headlines.name: No One Checked the Group Chat
headlines.flavor: A statewide presidents' meeting is rescheduled for the fourth time. Higher education continues unsupervised.

headlines.name: The Faculty Senate Agenda Is Nine Pages
headlines.flavor: Item one is approval of the previous minutes. It does not pass.
